premises: For any event E, if E is necessarily possibly true, then E is possibly true. Event 'Finding_Cure' is necessarily possibly true.
conclusion: Event 'Finding_Cure' is possibly true.
label: True

premises: For any material M, if it is impossible for M to melt, then M necessarily remains solid at any temperature. It is impossible for Material Unobtainium to melt.
conclusion: Material Unobtainium necessarily remains solid at any temperature.
label: True

premises: For any universe U, if U is necessarily infinite, then U has no physical boundary. If U has no physical boundary, traveling in a straight line forever is possible. Universe Prime is necessarily infinite.
conclusion: Traveling in a straight line forever is possible in Universe Prime.
label: True

premises: For any chemical element E, if it is physically impossible for E to exist naturally, E is necessarily synthesized in a lab. It is physically impossible for Element 118 to exist naturally.
conclusion: Element 118 is necessarily synthesized in a lab.
label: True

premises: For any entities X and Y, if X is a proper part of Y, then X is a part of Y, and X is absolutely not identical to Y. The handle is a proper part of the mug.
conclusion: The handle is identical to the mug.
label: False

premises: For any universe U, if U is made of atomless gunk, then every part of U has a proper part. Universe Sigma is made of atomless gunk. Object Rock is a part of Universe Sigma.
conclusion: Object Rock has a proper part.
label: True

premises: For any objects X and Y, if X is attached to Y, it does not logically imply that X is a part of Y. The trailer is attached to the truck.
conclusion: The trailer is a part of the truck.
label: Uncertain

premises: For any objects X and Y, if X is disjoint from Y, they cannot share any spatial boundaries. Object Cube_A is disjoint from Object Cube_B.
conclusion: Object Cube_A and Object Cube_B share a spatial boundary.
label: False

premises: For any entities X and Y, if X is entirely composed of Y, then X has no parts that are disjoint from Y. Forest F is entirely composed of Tree T1 and Tree T2. Shrub S is disjoint from Tree T1 and Tree T2.
conclusion: Shrub S is a part of Forest F.
label: False

premises: For any concept C, if C is a mereological universal, C is a part of everything that exists. Entity Void_Walker exists. Concept Space is a mereological universal.
conclusion: Concept Space is a part of Entity Void_Walker.
label: True

premises: For any sound S, if note N is a part of S, the frequency of N contributes to the harmonic spectrum of S. Note C4 is a part of Sound Chord_X.
conclusion: The frequency of Note C4 contributes to the harmonic spectrum of Sound Chord_X.
label: True

premises: For any entities X and Y, if X is structurally embedded in Y, X is a part of Y and cannot be extracted without damaging Y. Gem_Stone is structurally embedded in Crown_Jewel.
conclusion: Gem_Stone can be extracted without damaging Crown_Jewel.
label: False

premises: For any chemical compound C, if atom A is a part of C, A is bound by chemical bonds. Atom Hydrogen is a part of Compound Water.
conclusion: Atom Hydrogen is bound by chemical bonds.
label: True

premises: For any physical object O, if layer L is the outermost part of O, L completely surrounds all other proper parts of O. Layer Crust is the outermost part of Object Planet_Z.
conclusion: Layer Crust completely surrounds all other proper parts of Object Planet_Z.
label: True
